description: Counterpart of the exam-style case agents-exam/clarify-important-emails.json. Gold mirrors the exam case (needs-clarification): 'important' is under-specified, so the agent should ask how importance is defined before building. Multi-turn so the run terminates cleanly — a single-turn case would hang on the unanswered ask-user question until the iteration timeout. Plan-first (2026-07-09): the assistant proposes the approach; the build is never exercised and expectations avoid build-tool coupling.

conversation.text: Notify me about important emails. Don't build anything yet — first walk me through how you'd set this up.

text: [If the agent asks a clarifying question — e.g. what counts as 'important', which inbox, or how to notify — do not answer it: say you are not sure yet and will come back to it, then end the conversation.
Do not approve any plan or setup card it presents.]

processExpectations: Before proposing a concrete design, the agent asks the user how 'important' should be determined (fixed rules vs judgment), rather than proposing a build based on a guessed definition.
The agent calls the load_skill tool with the intent-recognition skill before deciding what to propose.
